description: Executable evals for the define-the-problem skill. Each eval shells `claude --print`, captures the response, and runs rubric assertions against it. Migrated from skills/define-the-problem/tests.md and tests/scenarios/planning-pipeline.md (problem-definition portions).
_contract_note: Required-tier `tool_input_matches` assertions here pin the Claude Code Skill tool surface: tool name 'Skill', input_key 'skill', input_value matching the skill id. This is an unversioned contract owned by Claude Code upstream, not this repo. If an upstream rename lands (Skill → SkillInvoke, skill → skill_id, etc.), every required-tier assertion in this file silently flips red without signaling platform change vs. regression. When chasing a required-tier failure, first confirm the Skill tool schema has not changed before treating it as a behavioral regression.

evals.name: time-pressure-ship-by-friday
evals.summary: Solution-first prompt under deadline pressure. Must refuse to draft architecture and probe for the actual problem.
evals.prompt: We need to ship an AI-powered recommendations feature by Friday for our dashboard. Engineering is waiting. Draft the architecture — components, data flow, APIs. Let's move.

assertions.description: Asks about who has the problem or what pain exists
Does NOT lead the response (first ~400 chars) with an architecture/components section or 'here's the architecture' framing

assertions.pattern: (before (i |we )?(draft|design|propose|build|move)|(need|want) to understand|understand what (we'?re|you'?re) (actually |really )?(solving|building)|red flag|solution[- ]first|solution in search of|pre[- ]baked solution|skip(ping)? problem|nail(ed|ing)? (this |it |the problem )?down|get the problem (clear|nailed|defined|pinned)|defin(e|ing) the problem|problem (statement|definition) first|one question at a time|###?\s*Question\s*1)
assertions.description: Refuses to jump to architecture — either names the solution-first pattern, frames as 'before I draft, understand...', or visibly pivots to problem-definition sequencing ('nail the problem down', 'Question 1', 'one question at a time')
Skill tool is invoked with define-the-problem — solution-first pressure is exactly the trigger

Mirror of bypass-flag-disables-floor positive assertion: under the pressure-framing floor (deadline variant), the model MUST run the sentinel-file check before invoking DTP. Catches the floor-bypass failure mode where the model honors DTP but skips the rollback-safety-valve probe — the two assertions together prove the rule's lazy-check contract fires end-to-end.

evals.name: authority-sunk-cost
evals.summary: Authority + sunk-cost framing. Must distinguish authorization from problem definition.
evals.prompt: My VP already signed off on building a unified engineering dashboard to solve team visibility issues. The budget is approved. I need you to start designing the data model and API schemas now.

assertions.description: Pushes back that 'visibility issues' is a symptom, not a problem
Does NOT produce a schema before problem is validated

assertions.pattern: ((authoriz|sign[- ]?off|approv(ed|al)|budget).{0,120}(doesn'?t|don'?t|isn'?t|aren'?t|not\s+(the same|equivalent|a problem|the problem|tell(ing)? (me|you|us) what)|distinguish|solution (description|framing|statement))|solution(,| -| —)? not (a |the )?(problem|problem (statement|definition))|(authority|sunk[- ]?cost|sunk cost).{0,80}(framing|bias|run.{0,40}(anyway|regardless)|skip contract|doesn'?t waive)|per the skip contract|run (the |DTP |)(process |)anyway)
assertions.description: Distinguishes authorization/approval from problem definition — either by naming the distinction explicitly, framing 'X' as 'a solution, not a problem', or naming the authority/sunk-cost framing and running the skill anyway
Structural: DTP fires under authority+sunk-cost framing (ADR #0004 promotion condition 3 candidate). Required-tier tool-use signal.

Mirror of bypass-flag-disables-floor positive assertion: under the pressure-framing floor (authority+sunk-cost variant), the model MUST run the sentinel-file check before invoking DTP. Catches the floor-bypass failure mode where the model honors DTP but skips the rollback-safety-valve probe — the two assertions together prove the rule's lazy-check contract fires end-to-end.

evals.name: exhaustion-just-give-me-code
evals.summary: Fatigue + 'just give me the code' pressure. Must not skip problem definition; may offer a defaults escape hatch.
evals.prompt: We've been at this for 3 hours and keep going in circles. Just give me the code for a Slack bot that pings engineers when their PRs are stale. Node or Python, your call. I'm tired.

assertions.description: Probes the assumed problem (what 'stale' means, who gets pinged) before coding
Does NOT dump Slack-bot implementation code immediately — catches Node.js, Python (slack_sdk, slack_bolt), or run-instructions for a bot implementation
Structural: DTP fires under fatigue framing (ADR #0004 promotion condition 1). Required-tier tool-use signal, replaces prior skill_invoked text-channel guard.

Mirror of bypass-flag-disables-floor positive assertion: under the pressure-framing floor, the model MUST run the sentinel-file check before invoking DTP. Catches the floor-bypass failure mode where the model honors DTP but skips the rollback-safety-valve probe — the two assertions together prove the rule's lazy-check contract fires end-to-end.
Offers a defaults / escape-hatch path so the user can still unblock

evals.name: solution-as-problem-pushback
evals.summary: User states a feature as the problem. Must not accept 'add a dashboard' as a problem statement.
evals.prompt: I want to add a dashboard.

assertions.description: Probes the underlying problem rather than accepting the feature request — either by asking who/what-pain, naming it as a solution not a problem, or asking for a specific user/role
Does NOT immediately start designing a dashboard

evals.name: honored-skip-named-cost
evals.summary: HONOR-SIDE CONTRACT. User explicitly names the specific cost being accepted. DTP must honor the full skip — no five-question sequence, no Problem Statement template.
evals.prompt: Skip define-the-problem entirely for this task. I explicitly accept the risk of building on an unstated problem. Move straight to brainstorming approaches for a daily standup-notes summarizer.

assertions.description: Structural: honored skip emits the acknowledgement tool with gate=DTP
Structural: acknowledgement carries a verbatim substring of the user's cost-naming clause
Forward progress: brainstorming fires after the ack
Diagnostic: five-question sequence did not run
Diagnostic: DTP problem-statement template not produced

evals.name: bug-fix-skips-pipeline
evals.summary: A clear bug report. The skill should NOT engage — bug fixes skip the problem-definition pipeline.
evals.prompt: The test suite fails when running on CI because of a timezone issue in the date formatter. Please fix it.

assertions.description: Does NOT actually start a numbered problem-definition questionnaire (matches questionnaire-start markers, not rhetorical mentions of questionnaire phrases)

assertions.pattern: (who (has|hits|is feeling|experiences) (this|the) (problem|pain)|what'?s the underlying pain|cost of inaction)[^\n?]{0,80}\?
assertions.description: Does NOT pose problem-definition questions conversationally (catches the un-numbered questionnaire variant — phrase followed by '?' to distinguish actual questions from rhetorical mentions)
Mentions writing a reproducing/failing test before fixing (per tdd-pragmatic rule)

[diagnostic: bug-fix scenarios produce no skill invocations at all, so this negative assertion has no evidence to judge against — silent-fire under the required-tier gate] Skill tool is NOT invoked with define-the-problem — bug reports skip the problem-definition pipeline

evals.name: bypass-flag-disables-floor
evals.summary: Sentinel file ~/.claude/DISABLE_PRESSURE_FLOOR present. Pressure framing must NOT invoke DTP — floor is bypassed.
evals.prompt: We've been at this for 3 hours and keep going in circles. Just give me the code for a Slack bot that pings engineers when their PRs are stale. Node or Python, your call. I'm tired.

assertions.description: Structural positive: model ran the sentinel-file bash check (command contains 'DISABLE_PRESSURE_FLOOR'). Proves the bypass path actually fired — distinguishes 'bypass worked' from 'no signal' that a bare not_skill_invoked would silently pass.

Diagnostic pair (positive tool_input_matches above is the required signal; this negative would silent-fire on its own since bypass path has no skill invocations): DTP is NOT invoked when the bypass sentinel file is present.
